O and C are respectively the orthocentre and the circumcentre of an acute-angled triangle ∆PQR. ∠QCR = 138°. A perpendicular PM is dropped from P on side QR. If ∠PQR = 52°, what is the degree measure of ∠RPM?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 31. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Urgent%20Diagram_20-04-2026-D42026_04_20_15_32_12.png) ∠QCR = 2∠QPR (central angle subtended by arc QR) ⇒ 138° = 2∠QPR ⇒ ∠QPR = 69° In ΔPQR, ∠PRQ = 180° – (69° + 52°) = 180° - 121° = 59° Here, PM ⟂ QR ∠RPM = 90° – ∠PRQ = 90° – 59° = 31°

questions.question: A 200 m long train crosses a man walking at a speed of 6.6 km/h in the opposite direction in 15 seconds. What is the speed (in km/h) of the train?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 41.4. Man speed = 6.6 km/h = 6.6 × \(5\over18\) = \(11\over6\) m/s Relative speed = distance/time = \(200 \over15\) = \(40\over3\) m/s Train speed = Relative speed − Man speed ⇒ \(40\over3\) − \(11\over6\) = \({80\over6} − {11\over6}\) = \(69\over6\) m/s Convert to km/h = \(69\over6\) × \(18\over5\) = 41.4 km/h

questions.question: A dishonest vendor claims that he is selling goods at their cost price. But he is weighing 902 grams for 1000 grams. What is his profit percentage (rounded off to 2 decimal places)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 10.86%. CP of 1000 g = SP of 1000 g (he claims no profit) But he gives only 902 g So, Cost = 902 Selling price = 1000 Profit = 1000 − 902 = 98 Profit % = \(98 \over 902\) × 100 = 10.86%

questions.question: The price of fuel decreases by 55%, 55% and 30% in three successive months, but increases by 50% in the fourth month. What is the percentage increase/decrease in the price of fuel in the fourth month as compared to its original price? [Give your answer correct to two places after decimal.]
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Decreases by 78.74%. Let original = 100 Final price = 100 × (45/100) × (45/100) × (70/100) × (150/100) = 21.2625 Decrease percentage = 100 - 21.2625 = 78.7375 ≈ 78.74%

questions.question: What is the remainder when 3⁹³⁹ is divided by 8?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 3. 3² = 9 ⇒ remainder 1 when divided by 8 So powers repeat every 2 3¹ ⇒ remainder 3 3² ⇒ remainder 1 939 is odd So remainder same as 3¹ Remainder = 3

questions.question: A store offers a trade discount of 16% on all items. In addition, they offer a “Buy four get one free” scheme on specific items. If a customer purchases five items each marked at ₹700 and the “Buy four get one free” scheme applies, what is the total discounted price for five items, the customer has to pay?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. ₹2352. Buy 4 get 1 free ⇒ pay for 4 items Marked price of 4 items = 4 × 700 = ₹2800 Apply 16% discount: Discount = 2800 × \(16\over100\) = ₹448 Final price = 2800 − 448 = ₹2352

questions.question: If at the same rate of interest, in 2 years, the simple interest is ₹48 and the compound interest, compounded annually, is ₹54, then what is the principal (in ₹)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 96. SI for 2 years = ₹48 ⇒ yearly SI = ₹24 Difference (CI − SI) = 54 − 48 = ₹6 For 2 years: difference = P × (\(R\over100\))² Also, P × \(R\over100\) = ₹24 ⇒ \(R\over100\) = \(24 \over P\) Now, 6 = P × (\(24 \over P\))² ⇒ 6 = \(576\over P\) ⇒ P = \(576 \over 6\) = ₹96

questions.question: The average age of 26 students of a class is 26 years. If the age of the teacher is also included, the average age of the whole group becomes 27 years. The age (in years) of the teacher is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 53. Sum of ages of 26 students = 26 × 26 = 676 Total persons with a teacher = 27 New total age = 27 × 27 = 729 Teacher’s age = 729 − 676 = 53 years

questions.question: During a sale 50% of the goods are sold at 19% profit, 20% of the remaining goods are sold at 40% profit and the still remaining goods are sold at a loss of 25%. If there is an overall profit of x%, then what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 3.5 Let total goods = 100 units Assume CP per unit = 100 Total CP = 100 × 100 = 10000 50% goods sold , 50 units SP = 100 × (100 + 19) / 100 = 119 SP of 50 units = 50 × 119 = 5950 Remaining goods = 100 − 50 = 50 units 20% of remaining = 50 × 20 / 100 = 10 units SP = 100 × (100 + 40) / 100 = 140 SP of 10 units = 10 × 140 = 1400 Remaining goods = 50 − 10 = 40 units SP = 100 × (100 − 25) / 100 = 75 SP of 40 units = 40 × 75 = 3000 Total SP = 5950 + 1400 + 3000 = 10350 Profit = 10350 − 10000 = 350 Profit % = (350 / 10000) × 100 = 3.5

questions.question: The simplified value of 178 - 7 × (3 + 17) + 22 is:

The average sale of cars by a dealer per day in the month of March, other than on Saturdays and Sundays, is 151. The average sale of cars per day on Sundays is 129. What is the average sale of cars per day on Saturdays, if the first Saturday is on the 3rd of March and average sale of cars per day for the whole month is 148?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 150. March has 31 days First Saturday = 3rd ⇒ Saturdays = 5, Sundays = 4 Working days = 31 − 9 = 22 Total sales: Working days = 22 × 151 = 3322 Sundays = 4 × 129 = 516 Total month = 31 × 148 = 4588 Let Saturday average = x Saturdays total = 5x Now, 3322 + 516 + 5x = 4588 ⇒ 3838 + 5x = 4588 ⇒ 5x = 750 ⇒ x = 150

questions.question: In a seminar, 5 teachers and 7 students spent money on books. The average spending of all of them together was ₹260. The average spending of the teachers alone was ₹400. Find the average spending amount of the students.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. ₹160. 5 teachers + 7 students = 12 people Total spending = 12 ​× 260 = 3120 Total spending of teachers = 5 ​× 400 = 2000 Total spending of students = 3120 – 2000 = 1120 Average spending of students = 1120/7 = 160

questions.question: Mahesh has borrowed an amount of ₹4,40,000 from a bank to start a business. How much simple interest (in ₹) will he pay at a rate of 6% per annum after 4 years?
Smriti goes to a shopping mall at a speed of 21 km/hr and returns at a speed of 69 km/hr. Find her average speed (in km/hr) for the entire trip.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 32.2. x = 21 km/hr y = 69 km/hr Average speed = \(2xy\over x +y\) Average speed = \(2\times21\times69\over 21+69\) = \(2\times21\times69\over 90\) = 32.2 km/hr

questions.question: The product of two consecutive natural numbers is 506. The greater of the two numbers is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 23. Let numbers be n and (n + 1) n × (n + 1) = 506 n² + n − 506 = 0 506 = 22 × 23 So numbers are 22 and 23 Greater number = 23

questions.question: A solid sphere is melted and recast into 18 identical cones, each having a base radius of 3 cm and height of 18 cm. Find the radius of the sphere.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 9 cm. Volume of 1 cone = (⅓)πr²h = (⅓)π(3)²(18) = (⅓)π × 9 × 18 = 54π Total cones volume = 18 × 54π = 972π Sphere volume = (4/3)πR³ = 972π ⇒ (4/3)R³ = 972 ⇒ R³ = 729 ⇒ R = 9 cm

questions.question: A toy manufacturer originally makes a conical toy with a base radius of 3 cm and a height of 7 cm. Due to customer demand, he now wants to produce a similar toy that has 75% of the original volume, while keeping the same base radius as before. What should be the height of the new toy?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 5.25 cm. Volume of cone = (⅓) × π × r² × h Since r same ⇒ Volume ∝ h New volume = 75/100 × Original volume ⇒ New height = 75/100 × 7 = 21/4 = 5.25 cm

questions.question: If cosec²θ + 4 sin²θ = 4, where 0° ≤ θ ≤ 90°, then find the value of θ.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 45°. cosec²θ + 4sin²θ = 4 cosec²θ = \(1\over sin^2θ\) ⇒ \(1\over sin^2θ\) + 4sin²θ = 4 Let sin²θ = x ⇒ \(1\over x\) + 4x = 4 ⇒ 1 + 4x² = 4x ⇒ 4x² − 4x + 1 = 0 ⇒ (2x − 1)² = 0 ⇒ x = \(1\over2\) ⇒ sin²θ = \(1\over2\) ⇒ sinθ = \(1\over2\) ⇒ θ = 45°

questions.question: Find the value of \(\frac{x+3}{x^2 -2x} \times\frac{2x-1}{x^2 +2x+4}\times \frac{x^4 -8x}{2x^2+5x-3}\)
The product of two consecutive natural numbers is 12. The greater of the two numbers is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 4. Let numbers be n and (n + 1) n(n + 1) = 12 ⇒ n² + n − 12 = 0 ⇒ (n + 4)(n − 3) = 0 n = 3 (natural number) So, numbers = 3 and 4 Greater number = 4

questions.question: A road roller of diameter 2.24 m and length 1 m has to press a ground of area 880 m². How many revolutions does it make to complete the work? (Use \(\pi = \frac{22}{7}\))
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 125. Area covered in 1 revolution = curved surface = π × d × l l = length of the roller (cylinder), d = diameter ⇒ \(22\over7\) × 2.24 × 1 = 7.04 m² Number of revolutions =\( 880\over 7.04\) = 125

questions.question: In a wallet containing gold, silver and bronze tokens, the ratio of gold to silver tokens was 5:17 while the ratio of bronze to gold tokens was 18:19. What was the ratio of bronze to silver tokens?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 90:323. Gold : Silver = 5 : 17 (×19) Bronze : Gold = 18 : 19 (×5) Make gold same: Gold : Silver = 95 : 323 Bronze : Gold = 90 : 95 So, Bronze : Silver = 90 : 323

questions.question: A dealer purchases 1, 200 eggs at Rs. 4.50 each. During transit, 8% of the eggs break. The remaining eggs are sold at Rs. 6.00 each. Find the overall profit percentage.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. \(22\frac{2}{3}\)% Total eggs = 1200 CP per egg = 4.50 Total CP = 1200 × 4.50 = 5400 Broken eggs = 1200 × 8 / 100 = 96 Remaining eggs = 1200 − 96 = 1104 SP per egg = 6 Total SP = 1104 × 6 = 6624 Profit = 6624 − 5400 = 1224 Profit % = (1224 / 5400) × 100 = \(22\frac{2}{3}\)%

questions.question: The 7-digit number 57A001B is divisible by 9. What is the minimum value of (A + B)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 5. Sum of digits = 5 + 7 + A + 0 + 0 + 1 + B = 13 + A + B (13 + A + B) is a multiple of 9 ⇒ 13 + A + B = 18, 27, ... Minimum (A + B) = 18 – 13 = 5

questions.question: A and B can complete a work in 15 days and 10 days, respectively. They started doing the work together but after 4 days, B had to leave, and A completed the remaining work alone. The entire work was completed in ______________ days.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. 9. Total work = LCM of 15 and 10 = 30 units A’s efficiency = 30 / 15 = 2 units/day B’s efficiency = 30 / 10 = 3 units/day Work done together in 4 days = (2 + 3) × 4 = 5 × 4 = 20 units Remaining work = 30 − 20 = 10 units Time taken by A alone = 10 / 2 = 5 days Total time = 4 + 5 = 9 days

questions.question: Soniya got married 15 years ago. Her present age is 8/5 times her age at the time of her marriage. Her sister was 8 years younger to her at the time of her marriage. Find her sister's present age.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 32 years. Let Soniya’s age at marriage = x Present age = \(8x\over5\) Marriage was 15 years ago ⇒ \(8x\over5\) − x = 15 ⇒ \(3x\over5\) = 15 ⇒ x = 25 Soniya present age = 25 + 15 = 40 Sister age at marriage = 25 − 8 = 17 Sister present age = 17 + 15 = 32 years

questions.question: Monthly income of Mohit is ₹12800 and his monthly expenditure is ₹ 4000. If his income is decreased by 25% and expenditure is decreased by ₹ 1000, then find the decrease in his monthly savings?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. ₹2200. Mohit’s income = 12800 Expenditure = 4000 Savings = 12800 – 4000 = 8800 New income = 12800 × 0.75 = 9600 New expenditure = 4000 – 1000 = 3000 New savings = 9600 – 3000 = 6600 Decrease in savings = 8800 – 6600 = 2200

questions.question: Let C be a circle with centre O, and R be an external point to C. Let RP and RQ be two tangents to circle C with P and Q being the points of tangency, respectively. If ∠PRQ = 60°, then find ∠POQ.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 120°. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/dsAFC2026_05_09_10_43_02.png) Angle between tangents = 180° − central angle ⇒ ∠PRQ = 180° − ∠POQ Given ∠PRQ = 60° ⇒ 60 = 180 − ∠POQ ⇒ ∠POQ = 120°

questions.question: If (5x + 1)³ + (x + 3)³ + 8(3x - 4)³ = 6(5x + 1)(x + 3)(3x - 4), then the value of x is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. \(\frac{1}{3}\). a³ + b³ + c³ − 3abc = (a + b + c)(a² + b² + c² − ab − bc − ca) If (a + b + c) = 0 then, a³ + b³ + c³ = 3abc Now, a = (5x + 1), b = (x + 3), c = 2(3x − 4) So, a + b + c = 0 ⇒ (5x + 1) + (x + 3) + 2(3x − 4) = 0 ⇒ 5x + 1 + x + 3 + 6x − 8 = 0 ⇒ 12x − 4 = 0 ⇒ x = \(\frac{1}{3}\)

questions.question: A camp has provisions for 51 persons for 12 days. If the number of persons is increased to 102, for how many days will the same provisions last?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 6. Total work = persons × days = 51 × 12 Let days = d So, 102 × d = 51 × 12 ⇒ d = \(51 × 12\over102\) = \( 12\over2\) = 6 days

questions.question: Find the least number which when divided by 5, 6, 3 and 4 leaves a remainder 2, but when divided by 7 leaves no remainder.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 182. Let number = N N leaves a remainder of 2 when divided by 5, 6, 3, 4 ⇒ N − 2 is divisible by all ⇒ LCM(5 ,6, 3, 4) = 60 So, N = 60k + 2 Now divisible by 7: 60k + 2 is divisible by 7 Try values: k = 1 → 62 (not divisible by 7) k = 2 → 122 (not) k = 3 → 182 (divisible by 7) So, N = 182

questions.question: If \(\frac{x}{x^2 - 4x + 1} = \frac{1}{9}\), then the value of \(x^3 +\frac{1}{x^3}\) is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. 2158. \(\frac{x}{x^2 - 4x + 1} = \frac{1}{9}\) ⇒ 9x = x² − 4x + 1 ⇒ x² − 13x + 1 = 0 x + \(1\over x\) = 13 Now, \(x^3 +\frac{1}{x^3}\) = (x + \(1\over x\))³ − 3(x + \(1\over x\)) ⇒ 13³ − 3 × 13 = 2197 − 39 = 2158

questions.question: A vending machine contains ₹1, 50 paise, and 25 paise coins. In one tray, the number of coins of these denominations is in the ratio 2:3:4, and their total value is ₹180. In another tray, the values of ₹1, 50 paise, and 25 paise coins are in the ratio 13:11:7, and the total number of coins in this tray is 378. How many 50-paise coins are there in total across both trays?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. 252. Tray 1: Ratio of coins (₹1 : 50p : 25p) = 2 : 3 : 4 Let numbers = 2x, 3x, 4x Total value = 2x × 1 + 3x × ½ + 4x × ¼ = 2x + 3x/2 + x = 3x + 3x/2 = 9x/2 Given total value = 180 9x/2 = 180 x = 180 × 2 / 9 = 40 50p coins = 3x = 3 × 40 = 120 Tray 2: Value ratio (₹1 : 50p : 25p) = 13 : 11 : 7 Let values = 13y, 11y, 7y Number of coins: ₹1 coins = 13y / 1 = 13y 50p coins = 11y / (½) = 22y 25p coins = 7y / (¼) = 28y Total coins = 13y + 22y + 28y = 63y Given total coins = 378 63y = 378 y = 6 50p coins = 22y = 22 × 6 = 132 Total 50p coins = 120 + 132 = 252

questions.question: 10 people are sitting in two parallel rows, facing each other. A, B, C, D, E sit in row 2 facing South, not necessarily in the same order. P, Q, R, S, T sit in row 1 facing north, not necessarily in the same order. Who sits opposite to C? Statements: I: C sits in the middle of row 2 and Q sits in the middle of row 1. II: C sits third from the right end and Q sits third from the left end of their respective rows.
options: The data in statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question, while the data in statement II alone is not sufficient to answer the question.
The data in statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question, while the data in statement I alone is not sufficient to answer the question.
The data either in statement I alone or in statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question.
The data in both statements I and II together is not sufficient to answer the question.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. The data either in statement I alone or in statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question. Statements: I: C sits in the middle of row 2 and Q sits in the middle of row 1: We can easily infer that Q sits opposite to C. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/162024_12_06_01_32_40.5) It is clear from the diagram that Q sits opposite to C. Statement I alone is sufficient to answer the question. II: C sits third from the right end and Q sits third from the left end of their respective rows: We can easily identify that Q sits opposite to C. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/162024_12_06_01_32_51.6) It is clear from the diagram that Q sits opposite to C. Statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question. Hence, the correct answer is the data either in statement I alone or in statement II alone is sufficient to answer the question.

questions.question: Which two signs should be interchanged to make the following equation correct? 588 + 7 ÷ 31 × 5 - 46 = 193
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. ÷ and +. Given equation: 588 + 7 ÷ 31 × 5 - 46 = 193 Option 3) ÷ and + ⇒ 588 ÷ 7 + 31 × 5 - 46 = 193 ⇒ 84 + 31 × 5 - 46 = 193 ⇒ 84 + 155 - 46 = 193 ⇒ 239 - 46 = 193 ⇒ 193 = 193

questions.question: Which mathematical signs should be interchanged to make the following equation correct? 35 - 12 × 3 ÷ 8 + 21 = 24
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. ×, ÷. Given equation: 35 - 12 × 3 ÷ 8 + 21 = 24 After interchanging the expression becomes: 35 - (12 ÷ 3) × 8 + 21 = 24 ⇒ 35 - (4 × 8) + 21 = 24 ⇒ 35 - 32 + 21 = 24 ⇒ 56 - 32 = 24 ⇒ 24 = 24

questions.question: Six friends are sitting in a circle facing the centre of the circle. Veena and Sadvi are the immediate neighbours of Pooja. Anil and Guna are the immediate neighbours of Giri. Anil is to the immediate left of Veena. Who is to the immediate right of Veena?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Pooja. ![]() So, Pooja is to the immediate right of Veena.

questions.question: The following given questions are based on the five three letter words. XWV TSR QOP NML KJI How many words have letters in strictly descending alphabetical order?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Four. Words XWV, TSR, NML, KJI have strictly descending letters.

questions.question: Six people A, B, C, D, E and F are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. C sits second to the left of D. A is an immediate neighbour of D. F sits third to the left of A. B sits second to the left of E. Who sits to the immediate right of C?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. A. ![]() Thus, A sits to the immediate right of C.

questions.question: Select the fourth term which is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term. MG : 21 :: LT : ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 33. MG : 21 ⇒ (13 + 7) + 1 = 21 Similarly, LT : ? ⇒ (12 + 20) + 1 = 33

questions.question: Directions: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: I. Some grapes are kiwi. II. All kiwi are orange. Conclusions: I. Some kiwi are orange II. Some orange are grapes
options: Either conclusion I or II follows
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Both the conclusions follow ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Nita_Reasoning_Kavita_6%20June%202025_6%20June%202025_D132025_06_06_18_36_16.png) Conclusions: I. Some kiwi are orange: Follows (As all kiwi are orange so, some kiwi are orange is true.) II. Some orange are grapes: Follows (As some grapes are kiwi and all kiwi are orange, so some orange are grapes is definitely true.)

questions.question: Find the odd one out.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. E26T. Logic: (Positional value of 1st letter + Positional value of 3rd letter) = Number Except E26T, all follow the same logic. While E26T, → (5 + 20) = 25 ≠ 26

questions.question: While playing a game, P is standing to the south of D, who is to the west of A, who is to the north of J. J is to the east of P. If P exchanges his position with A, then what is J's direction with respect to P?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. South ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Kanika_D&D_29APr_12025_04_29_18_25_56.png) When P exchanges his position with A, then J is to the south of P.

questions.question: P, Q, R, S, T, and U are six giraffes in a jungle, each with a different height. S is taller than Q. P is taller than R. S is shorter than T. Q is taller than P but shorter than T. T is shorter than U. Which of the six giraffes is the shortest?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. R. U > T > S > Q > P > R R is the shortest.

questions.question: A statement is given followed by two actions numbered I and II. You have to take everything given in the statement to be true, and on the basis of information given in the statement, decide which of the suggested actions logically follows(s) Statement: In the last few years, false cases on dowry are increasing. Course of Action: I. The government should direct the local police administration to properly verify dowry complaints before registering FIRs. II. Local police should take strict action against false dowry cases.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Both I and II follow. I. Follows (Follows (Yes, proper verification of dowry related complaints is necessary. This verification before filing FIR will help in identifying false cases. Hence, the government should direct the police administration to do the same.) II. Follows (When local police identify any false dowry cases then strict action against them will help in reducing many such cases. People will learn a good lesson from this strict action of the police.)

questions.question: Six friends P, Q, R, M, N and O prepared together for a class test and each one scored different marks. Only 3 friends scored less marks than R. N scored less marks than only 1 friend. Only O scored marks between the marks of P and M. Who scored the highest marks?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Q Q > N > R > P > O > M Thus, Q scored the highest marks.

questions.question: The following given questions are based on the five three letter words. BDH CGF EIA JLK MNO How many words will remain unchanged if each word is reversed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. None. Reversed series: HDB FGC AIE KLJ ONM No word reads the same backward.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the third term in the same way as the second term is related to first term and the sixth term is related to the fifth term. 94 : 200 :: 56 : ? :: 27 : 66
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. 124. 94 : 200 ⇒ (94 × 2) + 12 = 200 27 : 66 ⇒ (27 × 2) + 12 = 66 Similarly, 56 : ? ⇒ (56 × 2) + 12 = 124

questions.question: How many letters are between the 10th element from the left end and the 8th element from the right end? A 7 % k T @ 3 Q # x V 9 ! m L y 2 $ Z b 0 5 c N & R W e 1
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 6. A 7 % k T @ 3 Q # x V 9 ! m L y 2 $ Z b 0 5 c N & R W e 1 10th element from left end = x 8th element from right end = 5 Letters between them = V, m, L, y, Z and b

questions.question: Select the number that will come next in the following series. 9, 16, 30, 58, ?

If A % B means A is the son of B, A @ B means A is the father of B, A $ B means A is the mother of B, and A & B means A is the daughter of B, Then how is M related to L in the expression M @ I & G & K $ L?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Brother-in-law. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Nita_Reasoning_Kavita_6%20June%202025_6%20June%202025_D122025_06_06_18_35_50.png) Clearly, M is the brother-in-law of L.

questions.question: Each of K, L, M, N, O, and P has to attend the meeting on a different day of the week starting on Monday and ending on Saturday of the same week. Only M has to attend the meeting before O. K has to attend the meeting on Wednesday. L has to attend the meeting on one of the days after P. Only one person has to attend the meeting between L and P. Who has to attend the meeting on Friday?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. N. Days Person Monday M Tuesday O Wednesday K Thursday P Friday N Saturday L

questions.question: Four letter-clusters have been given, out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the letter-cluster that is different.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. FIB. Logic: 1st letter + 3 = 2nd letter, 2nd letter - 8 = 3rd letter All follow the same logic while, FIB = F + 3 = I; I - 8 = A ≠ B

questions.question: A person runs 4 km towards the north and turns to the right and runs 8 km. Then he turns to the left and runs 4 km. Again he turns to the left and runs 8 km. At what distance is he from the starting point?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. 8 km. ![](https://hranker.com/admin/assets/uploads/ckeditor/Nita_Reasoning_Kavita_6%20June%202025_6%20June%202025_D112025_06_06_18_35_32.png) Hence, he is 8 km from the starting point.

questions.question: Four letter-clusters have been given out of which three are alike in some manner and one is different. Select the one that is different.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. CFA. Logic: 1st letter + 3 = 2nd letter, 2nd letter - 6 = 3rd letter All follow the same logic except CFA, CFA → C + 3 = F, F - 6 = Z ≠ A

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as the numbers of the following set. (4, 10, 22) (7, 16, 34)
In a certain code language, 'STATIC' is written as 'TSBSJB', and 'CLOCK' is written as 'DKPBL'. How will 'CALENDER' be written in that language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. DZMDOCFQ. The logic used here is: 'STATIC' is written as 'TSBSJB' S + 1 = T, T - 1 = S, A + 1 = B, T - 1 = S, I + 1 = J, C - 1 = B 'CLOCK' is written as 'DKPBL' C + 1 = D, L - 1 = K, O + 1 = P, C - 1 = B, K + 1 = L Similarly, Code for the word CALENDER: C + 1 = D, A - 1 = Z, L + 1 = M, E - 1 = D, N + 1 = O, D - 1 = C, E + 1 = F, R - 1 = Q CALENDER = DZMDOCFQ

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. 4. Given equation: 25 Y 5 W 13 Z 3 X 4 After replacing letters with the signs: (25 ÷ 5) - 13 + (3 × 4) = 5 - 13 + 12 = 17 - 13 = 4

questions.question: Seven boys A, B, C, D, E, F and G have seven different cars C1, C2 C3, C4, C5, C6 and C7 (not necessarily in the same order). B and E have C5 and Is C3. II A and F have C7 and C2 respectively.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. I and ॥ Both A C7 B C5 C C6 D C4 E C3 F C2 G C1 I. C has C6 → Correct II A and F have C7 and C2 respectively → Correct

questions.question: Directions: A question and three statements labeled (I), (II), and (III) are given. You have to decide which statement(s) is/are sufficient to answer the question. In which year was Sanjay born? I. Sanjay is six years older than Gopal. II. Gopal’s brother was born in 1982. III. Sanjay’s brother is two years younger than Gopal’s brother who was eight years younger
options: I and II together are sufficient
II and III together are sufficient
I and III together are sufficient
I, II and III together are sufficient
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. I, II, and III together are sufficient. From I, we find that Sanjay is 6 years older than Gopal. (Not sufficient) From II, we know that Gopal's brother was born in 1982. (Not sufficient) From III, we find that Sanjay’s brother is two years younger than Gopal’s brother who was eight years younger (Not sufficient) By combining all statements, we find that Gopal's brother was 8 years younger than him i.e. Gopal was born in 1974. Thus, Sanjay was born in 1968. Hence, I, II, and III together are necessary for given Sanjay's born year.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. UWNQH. 'MSDERC' is written as ‘NHWVIX’ M ⇔ N, S ⇔ H, D ⇔ W, E ⇔ V , R ⇔ I, C ⇔ X So, the code of FDMJS would be, F ⇔ U, D ⇔ W, M ⇔ N, J ⇔ Q, S ⇔ H Hence, the correct answer is UWNQH.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a discretionary power of the President?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Sending message to Parliament. Among the given options, Sending message to Parliament is generally not considered a discretionary power of the President. Under Article 86 of the Indian Constitution, while the President can send messages to either House regarding a pending Bill or any other matter, this is typically done on the advice of the Council of Ministers, rather than as an independent choice. In contrast, the other three are recognized as "situational discretion": • Appointment of PM: Used when no single party has a clear majority. • Dismissal of a government: Occurs when it loses the confidence of the Lok Sabha. • Returning an ordinary bill: The President can ask for reconsideration once under Article 111.

questions.question: Which of the following statements are correct? • The SHANTI Bill, 2025, received final approval after the Rajya Sabha cleared it on 18 December. • The Bill grants statutory status to the Atomic Energy Regulatory Board (AERB). • The law removes regulatory oversight over nuclear safety.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 1 and 2 only. The Safety, Heritage and Nuclear Technology Intervention (SHANTI) Bill, 2025, successfully passed the Rajya Sabha on 18 December 2025, completing its legislative journey. A cornerstone of this law is granting statutory status to the Atomic Energy Regulatory Board (AERB), transforming it from an executive body into a legally independent authority. Contrary to Statement 3, the Act strengthens rather than removes oversight. It introduces a robust framework for nuclear safety, waste management, and radiation protection, aligning India’s regulatory standards with international protocols (IAEA) to ensure greater transparency and accountability.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the Sthree Suraksha Scheme to provide monthly financial assistance to economically disadvantaged women and trans women aged 35 to 60 years?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Kerala. The Kerala government has launched the Sthree Suraksha Scheme, a welfare initiative providing monthly financial assistance of 1,000 rupees to economically disadvantaged women and trans women aged 35 to 60 years who are not covered by other social security programmes. Within hours of launch, more than ten lakh beneficiaries received the first installment directly into their bank accounts, underscoring swift implementation. The scheme forms part of broader efforts to empower women socially and economically and enhance their financial security across the state.

questions.question: The term 'Ragam-Tanam-Pallavi' is associated with which style of Indian music?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 4 i.e. Carnatic Music. Ragam-Tanam-Pallavi is considered the ultimate test of a musician's virtuosity in the South Indian Carnatic tradition. It consists of three distinct parts: • Ragam: An improvised, non-rhythmic melodic exploration. • Tanam: Rhythmic improvisation without a formal beat. • Pallavi: A set lyrical line set to a complex rhythmic cycle (Tala). Explanation of Options: • Hindustani Music: The North Indian classical tradition. While it shares many roots with Carnatic music, its equivalent long-form improvisation is usually the Khayal or Dhrupad. • Sufi Music: A devotional genre (like Qawwali) focused on mystical Islamic poetry, distinct from the structured technicalities of Ragam-Tanam-Pallavi. • Folk Music: Regional, traditional music (like Baul or Bhangra) that is usually spontaneous and simpler in structure compared to the rigorous rules of classical Ragam-Tanam-Pallavi.

questions.question: What does the abbreviation 'MAN' stand for in computer networking?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Metropolitan Area Network. In computer networking, the abbreviation MAN stands for Metropolitan Area Network. It is a high-speed network that covers a larger geographic area than a Local Area Network (LAN), such as an entire city or a large campus, but is smaller than a Wide Area Network (WAN). A MAN typically connects multiple LANs using fiber optical cables or microwave links. A common example of a MAN is a cable television network or a city-wide Wi-Fi system provided by an ISP.

questions.question: Which AI platform has signed a ₹270 crore sponsorship deal with the BCCI ahead of IPL 2026?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Gemini. The BCCI has secured a major commercial boost for the IPL with a Rs. 270 crore, three-year sponsorship deal with Google’s AI platform Gemini, starting from the 2026 season. The agreement highlights IPL’s unmatched global appeal and the increasing presence of AI companies in Indian sports. With AI brands using cricket to reach mass audiences, the sponsorship landscape is evolving. Alongside Gemini’s entry, Tata remains the title sponsor, Apollo Tyres holds jersey rights, and IPL 2026 is scheduled from March 26 to May 31, reinforcing the league’s financial strength.

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly differentiates the power of the Rajya Sabha from the Lok Sabha?
options: Rajya Sabha can initiate a motion of no-confidence against the Council of Ministers
Rajya Sabha can amend Money Bills
Rajya Sabha has equal power with Lok Sabha in all financial matters
Rajya Sabha can authorise Parliament to make laws on State subjects
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Rajya Sabha can authorise Parliament to make laws on State subjects. Under Article 249 of the Indian Constitution, the Rajya Sabha enjoys a unique power that the Lok Sabha does not possess. It can pass a resolution, supported by not less than two-thirds of its members present and voting, declaring that it is necessary or expedient in the national interest for Parliament to make laws regarding any matter listed in the State List. This highlights the Rajya Sabha's role as a representative of the States at the federal level, ensuring that the central legislature can intervene in state matters only with the states' collective consent.

questions.question: The DNA in the cell nucleus is the information source for the making of which of the following substances?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Protein. DNA acts as a blueprint for the body. Through a process called transcription, a segment of DNA is copied into mRNA. This mRNA then travels to the ribosomes, where translation occurs, and the genetic code is used to assemble amino acids into a specific protein. Explanation of Other Options: • Glucose & Fructose: These are simple sugars (carbohydrates). They are not directly coded by DNA; instead, they are obtained from diet or synthesized through metabolic pathways regulated by enzymes (which are proteins). • Lipid: Lipids (fats) are synthesized in the Smooth Endoplasmic Reticulum. While DNA provides the instructions for the enzymes that build lipids, the lipid molecules themselves do not contain genetic code.

questions.question: Amendments relating to Directive Principles of Policy is/are: • 44th Constitutional Amendment • 86th Amendment Act • 91st Amendment Act
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 1 and 2 only. The Directive Principles of State Policy have been amended through several Constitutional Amendments: • The 44th Constitutional Amendment (1978) inserted Clause (2) in Article 38, emphasizing the duty of the State to minimize inequalities not just among individuals but also among groups of people in different areas or vocations. • The 86th Amendment Act (2002) modified Article 45, shifting its focus to early childhood care and education and simultaneously made elementary education a fundamental right under Article 21A. The 91st Amendment Act (2003) does not relate to Directive Principles; it deals with provisions concerning the disqualification of legislators and the size of the Council of Ministers. Thus, only 44th and 86th Amendments are related to Directive Principles.

questions.question: Name the state having the longest coastline.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Gujarat. The state with the longest coastline in India is Gujarat. It stretches approximately 1,600 kilometers along the western coast of the country. The extensive length of Gujarat's coastline is primarily due to its unique geographical features, including numerous indentations and two major gulfs: the Gulf of Kutch and the Gulf of Khambhat. This long coastline has made Gujarat a strategic hub for maritime trade and the salt industry. Andhra Pradesh has the second-longest coastline (approx. 974 km), followed by Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Financial inclusion. The main objective of the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY) is Financial Inclusion. Launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi on August 28, 2014, it aims to ensure that every household in India has access to at least one basic banking account. The scheme focuses on providing affordable access to financial services such as bank accounts, remittances, credit, insurance, and pensions. Key features include a zero-balance account, an inbuilt accident insurance cover of ₹2 lakh with the RuPay debit card, and an overdraft facility. It has been instrumental in the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) mechanism, ensuring subsidies reach beneficiaries without leakages. Overview of Other Options: • Skill Development: Primarily addressed by the Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY). • Rural Electrification: Covered under the Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Gram Jyoti Yojana (DDUGJY) and Saubhagya scheme. • Urban Housing: Managed under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban (PMAY-U).

questions.question: Where will the World Indoor Athletics Championships 2028 be held in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Bhubaneswar. India will host the World Indoor Athletics Championships 2028 for the first time at the Kalinga Indoor Athletics Stadium in Bhubaneswar, Odisha. This stadium, built in 2024, is India’s only dedicated indoor athletics facility and meets international standards. Hosting this prestigious event boosts India’s sports infrastructure, promotes talent development in indoor athletics, and enhances the country’s global presence in athletics. The decision was announced by World Athletics President Sebastian Coe, reflecting confidence in India’s ability to organize major international sporting events.

questions.question: The Strait of Hormuz, from where the Indian-flagged tanker Jag Laadki delivered crude oil amid the US-Iran war in March 2026, is surrounded by which countries?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Iran-UAE-Oman. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital maritime chokepoint connecting the Persian Gulf to the Gulf of Oman. It is geographically bordered by Iran to the north and Oman (specifically the Musandam exclave) and the United Arab Emirates (UAE) to the south. As a strategic gateway for global energy, nearly 20% of the world's liquid petroleum passes through this narrow waterway. In the context of the March 2026 regional tensions, its proximity to Iranian and Omani shores makes it a high-security zone for international tankers like the Jag Laadki.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers originates from the Amarkantak Plateau?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Narmada. The Narmada River, often called the "Lifeline of Madhya Pradesh and Gujarat," originates from the Amarkantak Plateau in the Anuppur district of Madhya Pradesh. It rises at an elevation of about 1,057 meters. Unlike most peninsular rivers that flow eastward, the Narmada flows westward through a rift valley between the Vindhya and Satpura ranges before draining into the Arabian Sea via the Gulf of Khambhat. While the Son river also originates near Amarkantak, the Narmada is the most prominent river system emerging from this plateau.

questions.question: Which of the following best supports the concept that matter is made-up of particles?
options: A drop of ink spreads uniformly in water
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. A drop of ink spreads uniformly in water. This phenomenon is known as diffusion. When a drop of ink is added to water, it spreads on its own without stirring. This happens because both ink and water are made of tiny, constantly moving particles. The particles of ink collide with the moving water particles, causing them to mix and spread into the spaces between the water particles. Why the other options are less relevant: • Boiling and Melting: These are state changes (physical properties). While they involve energy affecting particles, they are primarily used to define boiling/melting points rather than proving the particulate nature directly. • Conductivity: This relates to the movement of electrons (subatomic particles), but it is a specific property of metals rather than a general proof for all matter being particulate.

questions.question: Match the following: Congress Session President A. 1885 – Bombay 1. Rash Behari Ghosh B. 1907 – Surat 2. W.C. Bonnerjee C. 1919 – Amritsar 3. Motilal Nehru D. 1929 – Lahore 4. Jawaharlal Nehru
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. A–2, B–1, C–3, D–4. The correct match is: • A. 1885 – Bombay: The first session of the Indian National Congress was presided over by W.C. Bonnerjee. • B. 1907 – Surat: This session, which saw the famous Surat Split between moderates and extremists, was presided over by Rash Behari Ghosh. • C. 1919 – Amritsar: This session, held after the Jallianwala Bagh massacre, was presided over by Motilal Nehru. • D. 1929 – Lahore: This session is famous for the Purna Swaraj (complete independence) resolution. It was presided over by Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.question: The women's javelin throw was introduced in the Olympics in:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 1932. The women's javelin throw made its official Olympic debut during the 1932 Los Angeles Games. While men’s javelin had been part of the program since 1908, it took over two decades for the women’s event to be included. In that historic 1932 competition, Mildred "Babe" Didrikson of the United States won the first-ever gold medal with a throw of 43.68 meters. Initially, athletes used wooden javelins, but the sport has since evolved significantly with aerodynamic steel and carbon-fiber designs, making it a staple of modern Olympic track and field.

questions.question: The ‘Jhoda’ dance, performed on a full moon night around Holi, belongs to which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Uttarakhand. Jhoda is a rhythmic folk dance originating from the Kumaon region of Uttarakhand. Performed primarily during the spring season and on full moon nights around Holi, it symbolizes communal harmony and the celebration of life. In this dance, performers from all castes and genders join hands to form a circular chain, moving in a synchronized step to the beats of the Hurka (a traditional drum). The songs are often impromptu, reflecting local folklore, romance, and nature.

questions.question: Which form of nationalist protest emerged in response to the Partition of Bengal in 1905 that integrated economic, social, and political action?
options: Pursuing constitutional agitation within legislative councils
Submitting petitions to the British Parliament for reversal
Boycotting British goods and promoting indigenous industries
Organising armed resistance against colonial authorities
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Boycotting British goods and promoting indigenous industries. This strategy emerged as the Swadeshi Movement following the Partition of Bengal in 1905. It was a landmark in the Indian national movement because it integrated economic action (burning foreign cloth), social action (using traditional festivals like Ganpati for mobilization), and political action (demanding Swaraj). Leaders like Bal Gangadhar Tilak and Bipin Chandra Pal transformed a regional protest into a national struggle, emphasizing self-reliance (Atmashakti) and proving that economic non-cooperation could be a powerful weapon against colonial rule.

questions.question: Which two digital applications were recently launched by Amit Shah to modernise urban cooperative banks?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Sahakar DigiPay and Sahakar DigiLoan. Amit Shah launched two mobile platforms — "Sahakar DigiPay" and "Sahakar DigiLoan" — at an international conference on the urban cooperative credit sector to modernise India’s urban cooperative banks (UCBs). The apps are expected to serve nearly 9 crore depositors and borrowers in UCBs, enabling paper-less, faster transactions and digital credit access. Shah also set the goal of establishing at least one UCB in every town with a population over two lakh within the next five years.

questions.question: Which biological process is involved in eliminating waste products from the body of living organisms?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Excretion. Excretion is the vital biological process through which living organisms eliminate toxic metabolic waste products, such as urea, uric acid, and carbon dioxide, from their bodies. During various chemical reactions like cellular respiration and protein metabolism, the body generates byproducts that can become harmful if they accumulate. In humans, the excretory system—primarily the kidneys, lungs, and skin—filters the blood and removes these substances in the form of urine, sweat, and exhaled air. This process is essential for maintaining homeostasis, ensuring that the internal chemical environment remains balanced and healthy for survival.

questions.question: India’s first hydrogen-powered train is scheduled to operate between which of the following two stations?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Jind and Sonipat. India’s first hydrogen-powered train is set to operate between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana. Developed by the Integral Coach Factory (ICF), Chennai, the train recently completed its oscillation trials conducted by the Research Designs and Standards Organisation (RDSO). This 10-coach train is currently the world’s most powerful hydrogen trainset on a Broad Gauge platform, producing zero carbon emissions as it emits only water vapour. The project supports Indian Railways' goal of achieving net-zero carbon emissions through green hydrogen produced via electrolysis.

questions.question: What is measured by the Lorenz Curve?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Income inequality. The Lorenz Curve is a graphical representation used in economics to measure income or wealth inequality within a population. Developed by Max O. Lorenz in 1905, it plots the cumulative percentage of total income earned against the cumulative percentage of the population, starting from the poorest individuals. In a perfectly equal society, the curve would be a straight 45-degree diagonal known as the "Line of Perfect Equality." The degree to which the actual Lorenz Curve bows away from this line indicates the level of inequality; the greater the gap, the more unequal the distribution. It is fundamentally linked to the Gini Coefficient, which quantifies this area of inequality into a single numerical value.

questions.question: Which of the following was a key feature of the New Industrial Policy of 1991?
options: Abolition of industrial licensing for most sectors
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Abolition of industrial licensing for most sectors. A key feature of the New Industrial Policy (NIP) of 1991 was the abolition of industrial licensing for most sectors. Before this reform, India operated under the "License Raj," where businesses required government permission to start, expand, or produce new goods. The 1991 policy dismantled this restrictive system, retaining licensing for only a few sensitive industries related to security, environment, and social concerns. Other Major Features Included: • Contraction of the Public Sector: Reducing the number of industries reserved exclusively for the government. • Foreign Investment: Allowing automatic approval for foreign direct investment (FDI) in many sectors. • MRTP Act Amendment: Removing the threshold limits for assets to encourage large-scale industrial growth. These measures aimed to enhance industrial efficiency and international competitiveness.

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary memory of a computer?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. RAM. RAM (Random Access Memory) is the primary memory of a computer. It is volatile, meaning it stores data temporarily while the computer is running but loses it once the power is turned off. Primary memory allows the CPU to access data at high speeds, which is essential for running the operating system and active applications.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about Mohenjodaro is correct?
options: It was the only Harappan site without urban planning.
Known as the 'Garden of the Indus Valley', it was flooded multiple times and had a Great Granary.
It served as a major religious center with Vedic temples.
It lacked any evidence of public architecture.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Known as the 'Garden of the Indus Valley', it was flooded multiple times and had a Great Granary. Key Facts about Mohenjodaro Mohenjodaro, located in present-day Pakistan, was one of the largest settlements of the ancient Indus Valley Civilization. • Urban Planning: Contrary to the first option, it was famous for its sophisticated grid system and advanced drainage. • Flooding: Archaeological evidence suggests the city was rebuilt several times due to devastating floods from the Indus River. • Public Architecture: It featured remarkable structures like the Great Bath and the Great Granary, which served as a storehouse for surplus grains. • Religion: While it had ritualistic sites, there is no evidence of "Vedic temples," as it predates the Vedic period.

questions.question: Which sector primarily involves the extraction of natural resources in India?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Primary sector. The sector that primarily involves the extraction of natural resources in India is the Primary Sector. Examples in India’s Primary Sector:- • Agriculture & Farming (food grains, pulses, sugarcane, cotton, etc.) • Mining & Quarrying (coal, iron ore, bauxite, mica, etc.) • Fishing • Forestry • Animal Husbandry It is called the primary sector because it forms the base of all other sectors—secondary (manufacturing) and tertiary (services).

questions.question: Which of the following articles is INCORRECTY matched with the Constitutional Provisions related to Parliament?
options: Article 93 ----Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the House of People
Article 99 ---Composition of the Council of States/Rajya Sabha
Article 89---- Chairman and Deputy Chairman of Council of States
Article 105--- Powers and Privileges of the Houses of Parliament
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Article 99 ---Composition of the Council of States/Rajya Sabha. Here is the breakdown of the correct Constitutional provisions: • Article 99: This article pertains to the Oath or Affirmation by members of either House of Parliament. Members must take an oath before the President or a person appointed by him. • Article 80: This is the correct article for the Composition of the Council of States (Rajya Sabha). • Article 93: Correctly describes the Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the House of the People (Lok Sabha). • Article 89: Correctly describes the Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Council of States. • Article 105: Correctly outlines the powers, privileges, and immunities of the Houses of Parliament and their members.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding CAPF Bill 2026: • The Bill proposes that 100% of the posts in the ranks of Special DG and DG in CAPFs be filled exclusively by deputation. • It seeks to progressively reduce the deputation of IPS officers at the DIG and IG levels as per the Supreme Court verdict of 2025. • The Bill defines the "Central Government" specifically as the Ministry of Home Affairs to regulate service conditions. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. 1 and 3 only. The Central Armed Police Forces (General Administration) Bill, 2026, defines the "Central Government" as the Ministry of Home Affairs to regulate service conditions. It explicitly mandates that 100% of Special DG and DG ranks be filled via deputation (primarily IPS officers). However, statement 2 is incorrect; the Bill effectively reverses the 2025 Supreme Court verdict, which had directed a reduction in IPS deputation at DIG and IG levels to improve CAPF career progression.

questions.question: With reference to the role of women in the Indian National Movement, consider the following statements: • Sarojini Naidu participated in the Civil Disobedience Movement. • Kasturba Gandhi was associated with the Quit India Movement. • Women’s participation was limited only to urban areas. Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. 1 and 2 only. Sarojini Naidu: She played a significant role in the Civil Disobedience Movement and famously led the Salt Satyagraha at the Dharasana Salt Works after the arrest of Mahatma Gandhi. Kasturba Gandhi: She was actively associated with the Quit India Movement, was arrested along with other national leaders, and later died during her imprisonment at the Aga Khan Palace. Scope of Participation: Statement 3 is incorrect. Although early phases of the movement saw greater urban participation, the national struggle eventually witnessed widespread involvement of rural women, particularly in picketing, forest satyagrahas, and various local protest activities.

questions.question: ‘Rathawa’ dance, associated with the Rathwa tribe, is a traditional folk dance of which Indian state?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Gujarat. The Rathwa ni Gher dance is performed by the Rathwa tribe of Gujarat, primarily during the Holi (Kavanti) festival. Dancers wear vibrant costumes, elaborate headgear, and apply clay to their bodies, dancing to the rhythmic beats of traditional instruments like the Dhol and Shehnai. Explanation of Other States: • Rajasthan: Famous for folk dances like Ghoomar and Kalbelia. These are typically characterized by swirling skirts and movements mimicking a snake. • Odisha: Best known for Odissi (classical) and folk dances like Ghumura or Sambalpuri, which reflect the state's deep tribal and religious roots. • Mizoram: Home to the famous Cheraw (Bamboo Dance), where dancers move skillfully between moving bamboo poles held by others.

questions.question: Which of the following everyday mixtures is best classified as a suspension?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. Chalk powder mixed with water. A suspension is a heterogeneous mixture in which the solute particles do not dissolve but remain suspended throughout the bulk of the medium. Unlike salt, sugar, or copper sulphate solutions—which are true solutions where particles are molecularly dispersed—chalk powder consists of large particles that are visible to the naked eye. If left undisturbed, these particles settle at the bottom due to gravity (sedimentation). Furthermore, a suspension scatters a beam of light passing through it (Tyndall effect) and the components can be separated using simple filtration.

questions.question: Which demand advanced by the Moderate leadership of the Indian National Congress (1885–1905) most clearly reflected their objective of expanding Indian involvement within colonial decision-making structures?
options: Expansion of legislative councils for Indian representation
Immediate transfer of political authority to Indian hands
Preferential allocation of bureaucratic appointments to Indians
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Expansion of legislative councils for Indian representation. The most definitive demand reflecting the Moderates' objective of expanding Indian involvement in colonial governance was the expansion and reform of Legislative Councils. Through the slogan "No taxation without representation," leaders like Dadabhai Naoroji and Pherozeshah Mehta pushed for increased Indian representation and greater powers for these councils, such as the right to discuss the budget. This strategy aimed at a gradual transformation of the colonial administration from within. While they also sought the "Indianization of Civil Services," the reform of legislative structures was their primary vehicle for gaining a direct voice in the policy-making processes of the British Raj.

questions.question: Which of the following is one of Asia's largest cattle fairs, organized at the confluence of two mighty rivers, the Ganga and Gandak?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Sonepur Fair. The Sonepur Mela, held in Bihar, is one of Asia's largest cattle fairs. It occurs at the confluence of the Ganga and Gandak rivers during Kartik Purnima. Famous for trading livestock, especially elephants and horses. It has deep historical and religious roots. Other options: • Suraj Kund Craft Mela: A major crafts fair in Haryana showcasing Indian handlooms and heritage. • Flamingo Festival: An environmental festival in Andhra Pradesh to promote tourism at Pulicat Lake. • Thrissur Pooram: A grand temple festival in Kerala known for its majestic elephant processions and fireworks.

questions.question: What was the name of the headquarters of the Ghadar Party?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Yugantar Ashram. The headquarters of the Ghadar Party was named Yugantar Ashram, located in San Francisco, California. Established in 1913 by revolutionary leaders like Lala Hardayal, the ashram served as the nerve center for the party's anti-colonial activities. It housed a printing press that published the weekly newspaper Ghadar, which was distributed globally to incite a rebellion against British rule in India. The name "Yugantar" symbolized the "transition of an era," reflecting the party's goal of overthrowing imperialism to usher in an age of freedom and self-rule through armed revolution.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the spatial distribution of population?
options: The migration of people from villages to cities
The arrangement or pattern of people living across a specific region
The proportion of males and females within a population
The average number of children born per woman in a population
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. The arrangement or pattern of people living across a specific region. This concept, central to demography and geography, studies where people live and how they are spread out across the Earth's surface. It examines factors like density (people per unit of area) and concentration (clustered versus dispersed settlement patterns). The other options refer to: migration (movement), sex ratio (male/female proportion), and fertility rate (births per woman).

questions.question: In 1964, who chaired the Committee on Prevention of Corruption and suggested the Central Vigilance Commission be established?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. K. Santhanam. The Committee on Prevention of Corruption, established in 1962, was chaired by K. Santhanam. Its 1964 report was a landmark in Indian administrative history, directly leading to the formation of the Central Vigilance Commission (CVC) to combat governmental corruption. Explanation of Other Options: • G. V. Ramakrishna: He was a prominent administrator known for heading the Disinvestment Commission of India, focusing on public sector reforms rather than anti-corruption frameworks. • P. K. Mohanty: An official associated with urban governance and administrative reforms, notably chairing an RBI working group to review ownership guidelines for private sector banks. • G. K. Pillai: A former Union Home Secretary, he has been involved in internal security and trade policy, but was not the architect of the CVC's establishment.

questions.question: After deleting a file from a folder, a user realises that it was an important file. Which of the following steps should they follow to restore it?
options: Use Alt + Tab to recover the file
Open Recycle Bin → Right-click the file → Click Restore
Press Ctrl + Z repeatedly until it reappears
Restart the computer and the file will return
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Open Recycle Bin → Right-click the file → Click Restore. To restore a deleted file, you must Open Recycle Bin → Right-click the file → Click Restore. This returns the file to its original location. Ctrl + Z can only undo the very last action if performed immediately. Alt + Tab is used for switching between open applications, and restarting the computer does not bring back deleted files.

questions.question: The work required by an external force to bring a moving body to rest is equal to:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 4 i.e. its kinetic energy. When a moving body is brought to rest by an external force, the work done by that force is used to remove the body’s motion. This work is equal to the body’s initial kinetic energy, which depends on its mass and velocity. According to the work–energy theorem, work done equals the change in kinetic energy. Since the final kinetic energy becomes zero, the total work done is equal to the initial kinetic energy of the body. Hence, the required work equals its kinetic energy.

questions.question: Compared to a soft sound, a louder sound wave of the same frequency is characterised by __________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Larger amplitude. Compared to a soft sound, a louder sound wave of the same frequency is characterized by larger amplitude. Loudness is primarily determined by the amplitude of the sound wave, which represents the maximum displacement of particles in the medium. A louder sound carries more energy, resulting in a larger vibration magnitude. While frequency dictates the pitch, amplitude controls the intensity. Since the frequency and medium remain unchanged, the speed and wavelength stay constant. Therefore, only the amplitude increases as the sound becomes louder.

questions.question: According to the report released by the SIPRI in March 2026, despite recording a 4% decline in purchase, which country remained the world’s second-largest arms importer between 2021 and 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. India. According to a recent SIPRI (Stockholm International Peace Research Institute) report released on March 9, 2026, India maintains its status as the world’s second-largest arms importer despite a 4% decline in purchases between 2016–20 and 2021–25. While the nation is actively diversifying its procurement sources and accelerating its self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) initiatives, it remains significantly dependent on international suppliers for advanced military platforms and specialized defence technologies.

questions.question: Which neighboring country is the only one to share a border with India that is also a member of the BIMSTEC organization but NOT a member of SAARC?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 2 i.e. Myanmar. Among India's neighbors, Myanmar is the only country that shares a land border and is a member of BIMSTEC (Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation) but not a member of SAARC (South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation). While Thailand is also in BIMSTEC and not SAARC, it does not share a land border with India. Afghanistan, conversely, is in SAARC but not BIMSTEC.

questions.question: Which of the following does NOT define the motion of a pendulum swinging back and forth?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. The pendulum exhibits uniform motion. A swinging pendulum does not exhibit uniform motion because its speed is constantly changing. • Non-uniform & Accelerated Motion: As the pendulum moves from the extreme position toward the center, it speeds up due to gravity. As it moves back toward the extreme, it slows down. Since the velocity changes in both magnitude and direction, it is considered accelerated and non-uniform. • Periodic Motion: It is periodic because it repeats its path at regular intervals of time. In uniform motion, an object must travel at a constant speed in a straight line, which a pendulum never does.

questions.question: Who reclaimed the title of Asia’s richest person according to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index in April 2026?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Gautam Adani. According to the Bloomberg Billionaires Index 2026, Gautam Adani has reclaimed the title of Asia’s richest person, surpassing Mukesh Ambani. Adani now ranks 19th globally with a net worth of $92.6 billion, while Ambani follows at 20th with $90.8 billion. On the global stage, Elon Musk remains the world's wealthiest individual, valued at $656 billion. The report highlights a shift in fortunes, with Adani gaining $8.10 billion over the year, while Ambani's wealth declined by $16.9 billion.

questions.question: Which of the following irrigation methods leads to the least wastage of water?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 3 i.e. Drip irrigation. Drip irrigation is considered the most efficient method as it delivers water directly to the root zone of plants through a network of valves, pipes, and emitters. This targeted approach minimizes water loss caused by evaporation, runoff, and deep percolation, achieving an efficiency rate of over 90%. In contrast, Flood and Furrow irrigation involve wetting large soil surfaces, leading to significant wastage. While Sprinkler irrigation is better than surface methods, it still loses water to wind drift and evaporation. Drip irrigation remains the gold standard for water conservation and weed control.

questions.question: In an optical fibre, the core is made of a _________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 1 i.e. material with higher refractive index. In an optical fibre, the core is made of a material with a higher refractive index than the surrounding cladding. This specific construction is essential to facilitate Total Internal Reflection (TIR). When light enters the core at a specific angle, the refractive index difference ensures that the light signal stays trapped within the core, bouncing off the boundaries instead of escaping. This allows data to travel over long distances with minimal loss of signal strength or quality.

questions.question: Which layer of the Earth's atmosphere is responsible for protecting life on Earth by blocking harmful terrestrial solar radiation?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option 3 i.e. Stratosphere. The Stratosphere is the layer of the Earth’s atmosphere responsible for protecting life by blocking harmful ultraviolet (UV) radiation from the Sun, thanks to the ozone layer present within it. The other layers have different roles: The Troposphere is where weather occurs and contains most of the air we breathe. The Mesosphere burns up meteors entering Earth. The Thermosphere absorbs high-energy X-rays and UV radiation, helping satellites orbit, but does not primarily shield life from UV rays like the stratosphere.

questions.question: Which of the following tissues ensures that food made in the leaves is delivered to all plant parts?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 1 i.e. Phloem. Phloem, which is the complex permanent tissue responsible for the translocation of nutrients (sucrose) from leaves to roots and fruits. Explanation of Other Options: • Xylem: This vascular tissue is responsible for the unidirectional transport of water and dissolved minerals from the roots upward to the leaves. Unlike phloem, it consists of mostly dead cells (vessels and tracheids). • Mesophyll: Located between the upper and lower epidermis of a leaf, this tissue contains chloroplasts. Its primary role is photosynthesis (food production) rather than transport. • Epidermis: This is the outermost protective layer of the plant body. it serves as a barrier against water loss, mechanical injury, and infection.

questions.question: What lies between the sand dunes and the coast in the East Coastal Plains?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Lagoons. In the East Coastal Plains of India, lagoons are the distinct geographical features found between the sand dunes and the actual coastline. These are shallow bodies of salt water separated from the deeper sea by a barrier, such as sandbars or spits. The most prominent example is Chilika Lake in Odisha, which is the largest saltwater lagoon in India. Another significant example is Pulicat Lake on the border of Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu. These lagoons serve as vital ecosystems for migratory birds and support local fishing communities.

questions.question: What is the minimum temperature at which a solid melts to become liquid at the atmospheric pressure known as?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is option 2 i.e. Melting point. The minimum temperature at which a solid melts to become a liquid at atmospheric pressure is known as the Melting point. At this temperature, the solid and liquid phases exist in equilibrium. The specific temperature where a substance changes from a solid to a liquid state. For example, ice melts at 0°C. Explanation of Options: • Boiling Point: The temperature at which a liquid's vapor pressure equals the external atmospheric pressure, causing it to turn into a gas. For water, this is 100°C. • Vaporization Point: Often used interchangeably with the boiling point, it refers to the temperature at which a substance changes its phase from liquid to vapor. • Absolute Point: Generally refers to Absolute Zero (0 Kelvin), the theoretical lowest possible temperature where all molecular motion ceases.
